summary: Completed frontend-only e2e validation for thesis webapp interactions and responsive sanity checks across desktop/tablet/mobile.

ui_bugs.issue: Center premise node is not clickable; modal never opens.
Mobile horizontal overflow occurs (scrollWidth 433 on 390 viewport) due to keyword node extending outside viewport.

integration_issues.issue: Dialog content does not render after clicking center node; likely blocked interaction.

issues: Horizontal overflow present in hero keyword map
Keyword placement animation/transform exceeds viewport bounds

action_items: Fix ObjectiveTriangle center-node clickability so Dialog opens reliably.
Adjust hero keyword positioning/transforms for small screens to remove horizontal overflow.
Re-run targeted regression for modal + mobile overflow after fixes.
critical_code_review_comments: Probable click interception in ObjectiveTriangle: rotating decorative layer can block center trigger interaction (z-index/pointer-events issue).
App.css is large (>700 lines); split into module-specific styles for maintainability and safer responsive updates.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Most requested interactions pass after retest (left nav, top progress, challenge/literature/pipeline/objective highlight, RO1/RO2/RO3 toggles, integration, evidence filters, limitations, final panel). Remaining reproducible failures: objective center modal not opening and mobile overflow in hero keyword cloud.
rca of the issue: Objective modal bug RCA: Reproduced repeatedly by clicking [data-testid='objective-triangle-center-node']; no dialog nodes rendered (count=0). In ObjectiveTriangle, decorative .rotating-ring is rendered after center trigger and is absolutely positioned over the same area, making click interception likely. Mobile overflow RCA: hero keyword buttons use rotational translateY transforms that push at least one keyword node beyond viewport at 390 width (offender right edge=433).
